providerExtensions.name: MCP Servers Config (config.toml mcp_servers)
providerExtensions.description: MCP servers are configured in config.toml under the mcp_servers key, which is an object mapping server names to RawMcpServerConfig entries. Each entry supports: command (stdio binary path), url (HTTP/SSE endpoint), args (command arguments), env (environment variables), env_vars (list of env var names to pass through), env_http_headers (env var names for HTTP headers), http_headers (literal HTTP headers), bearer_token, bearer_token_env_var, cwd (working directory), enabled (bool), required (bool, fails session start if server is unreachable), startup_timeout_ms/startup_timeout_sec, tool_timeout_sec, enabled_tools (allowlist), disabled_tools (blocklist), tools (per-tool approval settings), oauth_resource, scopes (OAuth), and name (legacy display-name, kept for backward compatibility).

providerExtensions.name: Per-Tool Approval Mode
providerExtensions.description: Each MCP server can configure per-tool approval settings via the tools map in RawMcpServerConfig. Each tool entry (McpServerToolConfig) specifies an approval_mode that overrides the session-level approval policy for that specific tool. This allows fine-grained permission control per MCP tool without requiring global policy changes.

providerExtensions.name: MCP OAuth Support
providerExtensions.description: Codex supports OAuth authentication for MCP servers. Configuration fields include mcp_oauth_callback_port (fixed port for local HTTP callback), mcp_oauth_callback_url (custom redirect URI), and mcp_oauth_credentials_store (keyring, file, or auto). Servers can specify oauth_resource and scopes fields in their config. The OAuth credential store backend is OS-specific keyring by default with file fallback.

providerExtensions.name: Codex MCP Server Interface (codex mcp-server)
providerExtensions.description: Codex itself exposes an experimental MCP server interface via `codex mcp-server` (or `codex-mcp-server`). This runs a JSON-RPC 2.0 server over stdio using the MCP transport protocol, exposing v2 RPCs for thread management (thread/start, thread/resume, thread/fork, thread/read, thread/list), turn control (turn/start, turn/steer, turn/interrupt), account management, config read/write, model listing, and collaboration modes. It also serves v1 compatibility methods and sends live event stream notifications via codex/event/* channels. This is distinct from consuming MCP servers — it makes Codex itself controllable as an MCP server.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Tool Allowlist and Blocklist
providerExtensions.description: Each MCP server entry in config.toml supports enabled_tools (allowlist) and disabled_tools (blocklist) arrays. When enabled_tools is set, only the listed tools are exposed from that server. disabled_tools removes specific tools from the server's offering. This allows users to surface only the relevant subset of a server's tools.
